methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 11 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
RIOS Intelligent Machines is a robotics and manufacturing automation company that has raised $46M+ and was recently acquired. The valuation analysis draws from a sector universe of nine comparable companies—one public (ticker 277810.KQ) and eight private firms including 1X Technologies, Divergent, Exotec, Locus Robotics, and Bedrock Robotics.

The peer-median triangulation method is not applicable here. While eleven same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, none satisfy the filtering criteria for stage alignment, adjacency, or valuation-tier similarity required for robust median-based analysis. The narrow peer set lacks the minimum three datapoints needed within any filtered cohort to avoid misleading extrapolation from an overly broad all-sector median.

Given the methodological constraints, a reliable quantitative valuation range cannot be derived from the available data. To support a credible analysis, additional methods would require either: (i) expansion of comparable company data within the same valuation tier and development stage, (ii) transaction multiples from recent exits in robotics manufacturing, or (iii) discounted cash flow inputs specific to RIOS. The acquisition status of the target further limits forward-looking applicability. Key uncertainties include the degree of operational and revenue similarity between RIOS and the identified peer set, and whether public market conditions at acquisition time reflected current sector valuations.
